page: Microsoft Azure M16bds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MBDSMediumMemoryv3 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8473C processor running at 4.0 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled with a 410 GB local disk, and the instance is premium storage capable, optimized for block storage performance.

In benchmark testing, the M16bds_v3 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities. Its Geekbench single-core score of 2196.0 and text processing score of 2785.0 both rank in the top tier, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also perform strongly, with gzip compression reaching 32.97 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 13.74 GB/sec. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions support average prompt processing speeds, though text generation on smaller 135M models is strong at 303.34 tokens/sec.

The resource allocation profile of the M16bds_v3 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high memory density without the overhead of massive core counts. The inclusion of a 410 GB local SSD reduces the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. This configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, text processing pipelines, and software compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure M16bds_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the MBDSMediumMemoryv3 family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8473C processor running at 4.0 GHz, it provides 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance includes a 410 GB local disk and supports premium block storage. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits top-tier single-core performance, particularly in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. Multi-core operations, database tasks, and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. With its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, the M16bds_v3 is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, text processing, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M16bds_v3 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 410 GB local disk, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M16bds_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 410 GB local disk for database and memory-heavy tasks.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon cores, substantial RAM density, and integrated local storage for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8473C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GHz clock speed.
128.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Bundled 410 GB local disk with premium block storage capabilities.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks.
Advanced vector and matrix extension support including AVX-512 and AMX.
Optimized for memory-heavy databases, text processing, and software compilation.
